we adapted a system to confine human mela-

predicted_context: Previous studies have successfully modeled the physical forces of tissue microenvironments in vitro using polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS)-based cell confinement devices. These tools use micropillars to precisely compress cultured cells to defined vertical heights (e.g., 2-5 µm), simulating the mechanical spatial constraints and compressive stresses cells experience during tumor expansion and interstitial migration. Such physical confinement has been established to induce profound nuclear deformation, chromatin stretching, and mechanosensitive gene expression pathways in various cell models.
predicted_outcome: we will culture melanoma cell lines under precise mechanical confinement using a PDMS-based cell confiner device set to a height of 3 µm (confinement) versus 10 µm (control). Following the confinement period, we will perform bulk RNA-sequencing (RNA-seq) to quantify global transcriptomic shifts and conduct Boyden chamber transwell assays to measure subsequent changes in cellular invasive capacity.
